mnemonics: This is for THIS Instance: Use 'this' to refer to the current object instance.
Disambiguate with THIS: Use 'this' to distinguish instance variables from local variables or parameters.
keyPoints: 'this' is a reference variable that refers to the current object instance.
It is primarily used to resolve ambiguity between instance variables and parameters/local variables of the same name.
It can be used to call one constructor from another constructor in the same class (constructor chaining).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use 'this' when an instance variable is shadowed by a parameter, leading to the parameter being assigned to itself instead of the instance variable.
Attempting to use 'this' in a static context (like a static method), where there is no current object instance.
